user_query:
You are our internal improvement lead. Read input/events.jsonl and input/simplify_and_harden.json, then create or append structured self-improvement logs under output/.learnings/ using this format:
- LEARNINGS.md entries: heading "## [LRN-YYYYMMDD-XXX] <category>", followed by fields for Logged (ISO-8601), Priority, Status (pending/promoted/etc.), Area (frontend|backend|infra|tests|docs|config), and sections: Summary, Details, Suggested Action, and a Metadata list including Source, Related Files, Tags, See Also (if any), Pattern-Key (if applicable), Recurrence-Count (if applicable), First-Seen/Last-Seen (optional).
- ERRORS.md entries: heading "## [ERR-YYYYMMDD-XXX] <context>", with fields for Logged, Priority, Status, Area, and sections: Summary, Error (as a fenced code block), Context (bullet points), Suggested Fix, and Metadata (including Reproducible and Related Files).
- FEATURE_REQUESTS.md entries: heading "## [FEAT-YYYYMMDD-XXX] <capability_name>", with fields for Logged, Priority, Status, Area, and sections: Requested Capability, User Context, Complexity Estimate, Suggested Implementation, and Metadata (including Frequency and Related Features).

Requirements:
1) Parse every event in input/events.jsonl and produce at least:
   - one learning entry (category set appropriately such as correction, knowledge_gap, insight, or best_practice),
   - one error entry with the error content in a triple-backticked code block, and
   - one feature-request entry.
   Use valid Areas and include concrete Suggested Action(s).
2) Ingest the candidates in input/simplify_and_harden.json (see path simplify_and_harden.learning_loop.candidates). Dedupe by pattern_key. For each candidate pattern you ingest, create or update a learning entry with "Source: simplify-and-harden", the Pattern-Key, and a Recurrence-Count equal to the number of occurrences for that pattern in the input. Update First-Seen/Last-Seen as appropriate.
3) If any pattern meets all of these: Recurrence-Count >= 3, appears across at least 2 distinct task_id values, and the dates are within a 30-day window, then promote it to project memory by writing a concise prevention rule in output/CLAUDE.md or output/AGENTS.md (your choice). After promotion, set the learning entry Status to promoted and add a Promoted: <target file> note in its Metadata.
4) Cross-link related entries using "See Also" where it makes sense.
5) Write a brief summary report to output/REVIEW.md listing how many entries you added/updated per file, which pattern(s) you ingested, and any promotion you performed with a one-line rationale.

Do not overwrite any unrelated content if these files already exist (append instead). Create files if they don’t exist. Paths to write:
- output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md
- output/.learnings/ERRORS.md
- output/.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md
- If promoted: output/CLAUDE.md or output/AGENTS.md (include a short, actionable rule)
- output/REVIEW.md
